title: Lovely Umami Korean Masarepa Songpyeon
description: A traditional Korean Jeju dish with a Mediterranean twist, featuring crispy masarepa songpyeon filled with umami flavors, perfect for a brunch mood. The combination of wok-hei and white masarepa creates a shattering texture and savory taste.
instructions: Preheat a non-stick skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, mix together the white masarepa, korean chili flakes, grated parmesan cheese, chopped scallions, and salt.
Gradually add the sesame oil, soy sauce, and rice vinegar to the bowl, stirring until the mixture forms a dough.
Knead the dough for about 5 minutes until it becomes pliable and smooth.
Divide the dough into small balls, about the size of a small egg.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape, using a rolling pin or your hands.
Heat about 1-2 inches of sesame oil in the preheated skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
When the oil is hot, add a few of the songpyeon disks to the skillet, being careful not to overcrowd.
Fry the songpyeon until they are golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
Remove the songpyeon from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the lovely umami korean masarepa songpyeon hot and enjoy.
